title: Crispy Cornish Chicken Casserole
description: A citrusy, gooey, one-pot chicken casserole with currants, caramelized glaze, and a golden crust, perfect for a solo British dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C and place a copper pot inside.
Pat the chicken thighs dry with paper towels.
Season the chicken with sea salt and freshly ground black pepper.
Heat the copper pot over medium‑high heat, add olive oil, and sear the chicken skin side down until golden brown.
Flip the chicken and sear the other side until browned, then remove it from the pot and set it aside.
Reduce the heat to medium, add butter and minced garlic, and cook until fragrant.
Stir in the juice and zest of the lemon, fresh rosemary sprigs, and currants to coat the pan.
Pour in the coconut milk and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, allowing it to reduce slightly for about five minutes.
Mix the arrowroot powder with two tablespoons of water to create a slurry, then stir it into the pot until the glaze thickens.
Return the chicken to the pot, nestle it in the glaze, cover, and bake in the preheated oven for 25 minutes.
Uncover the pot, bake an additional five minutes to develop a crispy golden crust.
Remove the pot from the oven, let the casserole rest for five minutes, then serve hot.
